title: AM-GM flipped-direction counterexample
informal_statement: The flipped-direction claim '(a+b)/2 <= sqrt(a*b)' for positive reals (AM-GM actually says '>=') is false in general: a=1, b=9 gives (1+9)/2 = 5 > 3 = sqrt(1*9).
informal_proof_idea: Witness a=1, b=9; rewrite 1*9 as 3^2 and apply Real.sqrt_sq, then norm_num closes the numeric inequality 5 > 3. Companion to the negative example negative.inequalities.amgm_wrong_direction_bare_nlinarith_failure.v1, which shows a natural nlinarith attempt on the flipped statement genuinely fails (as it must, since the statement is false).
formal_statement_pp: theorem amgm_wrong_direction_counterexample : ∃ (a b : ℝ), a > 0 ∧ b > 0 ∧ (a + b) / 2 > Real.sqrt (a * b)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 5c239fdd). Falseness of the flipped AM-GM direction is kernel-verified directly via this explicit witness, complementing the failed universal-claim attempt preserved as negative.inequalities.amgm_wrong_direction_bare_nlinarith_failure.v1 (a separate episode, ade3dee6). Resolves the queued 'AM-GM in the wrong direction' candidate in packets/negative/inequalities/QUEUE.md.
